title: Functions should not be defined inside loops
description: Defining functions inside loops in JavaScript can lead to several issues and is generally considered bad practice. The main problems associated with this approach are related to performance, scope, and potential unintended behavior: When a function is defined inside a loop, the function is re-created on each iteration of the loop. This can cause unnecessary overhead and lead to performance issues, especially if the loop runs repeatedly.
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

checks.message: Function declaration found inside for loop. Move function outside loop to avoid recreation on each iteration.
Function declaration found inside while loop. Move function outside loop to avoid recreation on each iteration.
Function expression assigned inside for loop. Consider moving function definition outside loop.
Function expression assigned inside while loop. Consider moving function definition outside loop.
Arrow function assigned inside for loop. Consider moving function definition outside loop.
Function defined inside for-in/for-of loop. Move function outside loop for better performance.
Function defined inside do-while loop. Move function outside loop to avoid recreation.
